title: Developer Tools and SDK
description: Developer-facing tooling: Puter SDK API logging, backend service registration, release note generation, and self-hosted setup orchestration.

use_cases.name: Call Puter API via SDK
use_cases.description: Client code calls any Puter service operation through the puter-js SDK layer.

use_cases.name: Enable / Disable API Call Logging
use_cases.description: Developer toggles logging on/off via APICallLogger.enable() / disable() or updateConfig().

use_cases.name: Log API Request
use_cases.description: On each SDK call, logRequest({service, operation, params, result, error}) emits a formatted console line. Triggered automatically when logging is enabled.

use_cases.name: Get Logger Stats
use_cases.description: Developer queries APICallLogger.getStats() to inspect enabled state and current config.

use_cases.name: Register API Routes
use_cases.description: On service boot, PuterAPIService.__on_install.routes() mounts all REST routers (auth, apps, kvstore, drivers, healthcheck, etc.) onto the Express app retrieved from 'web-server' service.

use_cases.name: Register a Service
use_cases.description: ServiceManager.register(name, factory, options) instantiates a service and places it into Registering → Pending or Initializing state depending on dependency readiness.

use_cases.name: Resolve Service Dependencies
use_cases.description: ServiceManager.maybe_init_() checks TService.get_depends(); if satisfied it calls init_service_(), otherwise marks entry Pending and enqueues it in waiting_ map.

use_cases.name: Wait for Service Initialization
use_cases.description: ServiceManager.aget(name) or wait_for_init(depends) blocks until all listed services reach Running status via init_listeners_ callbacks.

use_cases.name: Generate Release Notes
use_cases.description: Tool reads git log between two version tags (params.from / params.to), parses conventional commits with CC_REGEX, applies complicated_cases transformers and retro_prefixes overrides, groups by scope+type, and outputs Markdown changelog to stdout.

use_cases.name: Run Self-Hosted Backend
use_cases.description: Operator runs the script; it instantiates a Kernel, adds EssentialModules + DatabaseModule + LocalDiskStorageModule + SelfHostedModule + BroadcastModule + TestDriversModule + TestConfigModule + InternetModule, then calls k.boot() to start the full stack.

use_cases.name: Configure Self-Hosted Environment
use_cases.description: Optional dotenv loading (dotenv/config) at startup allows environment variable overrides before Kernel boot.

key_components.name: PuterAPIService
responsibilities: Mounts 40+ Express routers (auth, apps, kvstore, drivers, user management)

key_components.name: APICallLogger
responsibilities: Formats log as: service - operation - params → result
Differentiates error vs success log output

key_components.name: ServiceManager
responsibilities: Async get with await (aget) blocks until service Running
init_listeners_ callbacks notify dependents after init

key_components.name: gen-release-notes.js
key_logic: complicated_cases transformers: fix_i18n, deps_scope, puterai_is_ai, doc_scopes
retro_prefixes overrides for legacy non-conventional commits
Outputs Markdown grouped by scope then type (feat, fix, i18n)

key_components.name: run-selfhosted.js
key_logic: Adds modules: EssentialModules[], DatabaseModule, LocalDiskStorageModule, SelfHostedModule, BroadcastModule, TestDriversModule, TestConfigModule, InternetModule

key_relationships: ServiceManager manages lifecycle of PuterAPIService and all other services
APICallLogger used by puter-js SDK to wrap every outbound API call
run-selfhosted.js composes Kernel with multiple Modules including one that registers PuterAPIService
gen-release-notes.js depends_on simple-git for commit history
important_notes: PuterAPIService route installation is event-driven: triggered by '__on_install.routes' lifecycle event, not constructor
ServiceManager uses dependency graph via TService.get_depends() — circular deps would deadlock
APICallLogger is opt-in (enabled: false by default); must call enable() or pass {enabled: true} in constructor
gen-release-notes hardcodes version params (from/to/date) — meant to be edited before each release run
run-selfhosted.js uses import.meta.filename polyfill for Node version compatibility
